 {"id":"bd-11wv","title":"Bubbletea: track async event thread via TaskTracker","description":"# Bubbletea Async Event Thread Tracking

## Problem
In program.rs, the async event loop path (run_async / async_event_loop) spawns
an event polling thread at line ~1109:

    std::thread::spawn(move || {
        loop {
            if event_cancel.is_cancelled() { break; }
            match event::poll(Duration::from_millis(100)) { ... }
        }
    });

This thread uses a CancellationToken to know when to stop, but it is NOT
spawned via task_tracker.spawn_blocking(). The graceful shutdown sequence is:

1. cancel_token.cancel()          // signals all tracked tasks
2. task_tracker.close()           // prevents new tasks
3. task_tracker.wait() with 5s timeout  // waits for tracked tasks

Since the event thread is NOT tracked, step 3 doesn't wait for it. The thread
may still be blocked in event::poll() or event::read() when the async runtime
shuts down. This creates a race: the event thread may try to send on a dropped
tokio channel, causing a panic or silent failure.

## Why It Matters
- Graceful shutdown is incomplete — the event thread outlives the runtime
- In production SSH servers, accumulated leaked threads = resource exhaustion
- The 100ms poll timeout means the thread *usually* stops quickly, but "usually"
  is not "always" — event::read() can block indefinitely on some platforms

## Solution
Replace std::thread::spawn() with task_tracker.spawn_blocking() which:
- Registers the thread with TaskTracker
- Ensures graceful shutdown waits for it
- Proper cancellation via the existing CancellationToken

The spawn_blocking signature:
    task_tracker.spawn_blocking(move || { ... });

This is a minimal change — the thread body stays identical, only the spawn
mechanism changes.

## Key Files
- crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs (async event loop, line ~1109)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Event thread spawned via task_tracker.spawn_blocking()
- Graceful shutdown waits for event thread
- CancellationToken still used for early exit
- Existing tests pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:37:47.300659380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T23:49:00.237212897Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11wv","depends_on_id":"bd-1s5p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:37:47.323443511Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":34,"issue_id":"bd-11wv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETION CRITERIA: This work stream is complete when subtask is done:
1. bd-2353: Spawn via task_tracker.spawn_blocking
Plus the testing bead bd-2i18 must pass.","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:32:26Z"},{"id":49,"issue_id":"bd-11wv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:
bd-2353 is a SINGLE LINE CHANGE:

Before: std::thread::spawn(move || { ... });
After:  task_tracker.spawn_blocking(move || { ... });

The task_tracker variable is already in scope at line 1110.

CRITICAL PATH: bd-2353 → bd-2i18 → bd-1azg","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:49:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-11zs","title":"Property Tests: lipgloss dimension calculations","description":"Property-based tests for lipgloss width/height calculations.

## Purpose
Use proptest to verify dimension calculation invariants
across all style combinations.

## Properties to Test

### Width Invariants
- [ ] output_width >= content_width (when width set)
- [ ] output_width == width when width > content_width
- [ ] padding adds exactly padding_left + padding_right
- [ ] margin adds exactly margin_left + margin_right

### Height Invariants
- [ ] output_height >= content_lines
- [ ] padding adds exactly padding_top + padding_bottom
- [ ] border adds exactly 2 (top + bottom) when present

### Unicode Width
- [ ] Wide chars (CJK) count as 2
- [ ] Combining chars count as 0
- [ ] Emoji width handled correctly
- [ ] ANSI sequences don't affect width

### Composition
- [ ] inherit() preserves dimension calculations
- [ ] copy() produces identical dimensions
- [ ] nested styles compute correctly

## Test Strategy

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn width_never_less_than_content(
        content in "[\\w\\s]{0,100}",
        width in 0usize..200,
        padding in 0usize..20,
    ) {
        let style = Style::new()
            .width(width)
            .padding_horizontal(padding);
        let output = style.render(&content);
        assert!(visual_width(&output) >= visual_width(&content));
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria
- Dimensions always calculated correctly
- Unicode edge cases handled
- No panics for any style combination","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:06.723939917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:26:51.659766118Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:26:51.659630014Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lipgloss","property","proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11zs","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:57.783744084Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-11zs","depends_on_id":"bd-ndn1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:06.743725346Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1327","title":"Bubbletea: unit tests for sync thread lifecycle","description":"# Unit Tests for Sync Thread Lifecycle

## Purpose
Verify that the sync event loop properly captures JoinHandles and joins threads
on shutdown. These tests ensure the thread lifecycle fixes actually work.

## Test Cases

### 1. test_thread_handles_captured
Verify that all three spawned threads (external forwarder, input parser, command
executor) return JoinHandles that can be stored.

### 2. test_threads_exit_on_channel_drop
Verify that threads exit when their input channels are dropped.

### 3. test_shutdown_joins_all_threads
Integration test using a mock Program that spawns threads and verifies all
join within timeout.

### 4. test_join_timeout_logs_warning
Verify that if a thread doesnt join within timeout, a warning is logged.

## Logging Requirements
All tests should use tracing_test::traced_test to capture log output and
verify expected log messages appear.

## Key File
- crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs (add tests to existing test module)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 4 test cases implemented and passing
- Tests use tracing_test for log verification
- Tests are deterministic (no flaky timing issues)
- Tests document expected behavior in comments","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:28:34.479166183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T01:16:36.481189991Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1327","depends_on_id":"bd-1dsp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:28:34.495744040Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1327","depends_on_id":"bd-3azk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:29:44.784071319Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":38,"issue_id":"bd-1327","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"NOTE: Test case 4 (test_join_timeout_logs_warning) should be renamed to test_slow_thread_join_logs_debug since we dont implement explicit timeout. The test verifies:
1. Thread joins eventually complete
2. Debug logging shows join progress
3. If a thread panics, warn logging shows the panic

Rename test case 4 to: test_thread_panic_logs_warning
Verify: match handle.join() logs Err case with tracing::warn","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:46:48Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-14v4","title":"demo_showcase core: navigation sidebar (List + filtering)","description":"Build a polished navigation sidebar that makes the multi-page app feel cohesive.

  - escape clears filter and restores full list
- Mouse (optional):

Integration:
- Navigation emits a single clean app-level route message (no page-to-page coupling).

- Switching pages feels instant.
- Filtering nav demonstrates textinput+list integration and feels fast.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:58.826538621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:48:51.210183012Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:48:51.210116268Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-14v4","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:19.445789703Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-14v4","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:18.267533574Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-14v4","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:58.851229842Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1bja","title":"charmed_log: Handle RwLock poisoning gracefully instead of panic","description":"# Bug: RwLock Poisoning Causes Application-Wide Crash

## Problem Statement
The charmed_log crate uses .unwrap() on all RwLock read/write acquisitions.
If any thread panics while holding the lock, the lock becomes "poisoned" and
ALL future lock acquisitions will panic, cascading to crash the entire application.

## Affected Code Locations
```
Line 427: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 468: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 475: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 481: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 488: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 494: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 500: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 506: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 512: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 518: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 528: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 579: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 597: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
```

## Root Cause Analysis
The pattern `self.inner.read().unwrap()` assumes the lock will never be poisoned.
However, if a thread panics during:
- Format string processing (e.g., invalid UTF-8)
- Style rendering (e.g., lipgloss issue)
- Any code path holding the lock

Then ALL subsequent logging calls crash with:
`called Result::unwrap() on an Err value: PoisonError { ... }`

## Solution Design
Replace all .unwrap() with .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) pattern:

```rust
// Before (panics on poisoned lock):
let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();

// After (recovers from poisoning):
let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
```

This pattern:
1. Recovers the inner data even if lock was poisoned
2. Allows logging to continue functioning
3. Is the recommended pattern for "must not fail" operations like logging

## Alternative Considered: Return Result
Changing the API to return Result<(), LogError> was considered but rejected because:
- Breaking API change
- Logging should be fire-and-forget
- Callers shouldn't need to handle logging errors in their business logic

## Implementation Steps
1. Create helper macro or function to encapsulate the unwrap_or_else pattern
2. Replace all 13 occurrences
3. Add test that verifies logging continues after simulated panic
4. Document the recovery behavior

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn logging_continues_after_panic_in_another_thread() {
    let logger = Logger::new();
    
    // Spawn thread that panics while holding lock
    let logger_clone = logger.clone();
    std::thread::spawn(move || {
        // Acquire lock and panic
        // (requires test-only hook to simulate)
    }).join().ok();
    
    // This should NOT panic - logging should continue
    logger.info("After panic", &[]);
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 13 .unwrap() calls replaced with poison-recovery pattern
- [ ] Test demonstrates continued operation after poisoning
- [ ] No performance regression (unwrap_or_else is zero-cost when lock is healthy)
- [ ] Documentation updated to note recovery behavior","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:15:34.303042431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:26:49.131050035Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:26:49.130977760Z","close_reason":"Already implemented - all RwLock accesses use unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) pattern","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","critical","panic-fix","thread-safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1bja","depends_on_id":"bd-3ju1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:15:34.329172028Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1cgb","title":"EPIC: E2E Test Coverage with Detailed Logging","description":"End-to-end tests for complete application workflows with comprehensive logging.

## Philosophy
- Test full user workflows, not just units
- Detailed logging for debugging test failures
- Capture terminal state at each step
- Support both headless and interactive modes

## Target Applications
1. demo_showcase - primary E2E target
   - All 8 pages (Dashboard, Services, Jobs, Logs, Docs, Files, Wizard, Settings)
   - Navigation flows (sidebar, keyboard shortcuts)
   - Theme switching and persistence
   - Shell-out and restore patterns
   - Wizard deployment workflow

2. glow - markdown viewer
   - File loading and rendering
   - Navigation and search
   - Stash operations

## Logging Requirements
- Timestamp every test step
- Log all key presses and expected outcomes
- Capture full terminal state on failure
- Include ANSI sequence dumps for debugging
- Log timing information for performance tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:54.027535577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:14:46.025801319Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:14:46.025720748Z","close_reason":"All child tasks completed: logging framework (bd-28kk), shell-out tests (bd-3ilg), wizard workflow (bd-3nit), navigation flows (bd-2zj3), logs filtering (bd-1s7t), theme/settings (bd-21sm). E2E Test Coverage epic complete.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cgb","depends_on_id":"bd-665u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:54.034668586Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":24,"issue_id":"bd-1cgb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"E2E LOGGING REQUIREMENTS - Non-Negotiable:

Every E2E test MUST include detailed logging that makes debugging failures possible
without re-running the test interactively. This is critical for CI/CD reliability.

REQUIRED LOG CONTENT:
1. Test identification (name, timestamp, environment)
2. Each test step with description
3. All input events (key presses, mouse clicks)
4. Expected outcome for each assertion
5. Actual outcome (pass/fail with details)
6. Terminal state capture on failure
7. Timing information for performance analysis

LOG FORMAT EXAMPLE:
```
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.123Z] TEST_START: test_wizard_deployment_flow
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.124Z] ENV: TERM=xterm-256color, COLORTERM=truecolor
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.125Z] STEP: Initialize application
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.130Z] STEP: Navigate to Wizard page (key: '7')
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.131Z] INPUT: KeyEvent { code: Char('7'), modifiers: NONE }
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.135Z] ASSERT: current_page == Page::Wizard
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.135Z] RESULT: PASS
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.136Z] STEP: Enter service name 'test-service'
...
```

The infrastructure task (bd-28kk) MUST be completed first - all other E2E tests
depend on it for consistent logging.","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dr6","title":"demo_showcase page: Jobs/Tasks (async workflows, progress, filtering)","description":"A realistic CI/job-runner screen: a dense jobs list/table with progress, filtering, and actions that feel like a real ops console.

Why:
- This page is the best “integration crucible” for the stack:
  - bubbletea message routing + command composition
  - bubbles components (table/list, progress, spinner, paginator, timer/stopwatch)
  - harmonica motion for compelling progress/counter animation (optional)
  - charmed_log/toasts for outcomes and errors
- It proves the architecture scales beyond toy demos.

Must showcase (minimum):
- Navigation + selection:
  - keyboard-first (arrows/jk, enter), consistent focus model
  - selection stability across refreshes and filter changes
- Status + progress:
  - determinate progress bars and indeterminate spinners
  - color-coded statuses (running/succeeded/failed/cancelled)
  - durations + ETA-ish hints (timer/stopwatch)
- Realistic operations:
  - start/cancel/retry actions implemented via Cmd/AsyncCmd
  - action results visible (toast + log + UI state)
- Discoverability:
  - query bar + filtering + sorting
  - paginator for large lists
- Details pane:
  - shows job metadata, last logs, parameters, timeline / steps

Data requirements:
- Driven by the deterministic simulation engine and seeded generator.
- Integrates with Wizard: wizard triggers new jobs; jobs correlate to logs.

Acceptance:
- Feels like an actual CI dashboard, not a toy.
- Actions are responsive: input never blocks, state updates are obvious.
- Filtering is fast and predictable.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - filtering/sorting correctness and stability
  - action state machine (start/cancel/retry) transitions
  - progress rendering decisions (spinner vs bar, colors)
- E2E:
  - wizard -> job -> logs correlation scenario
  - smoke tour coverage
  - optional “jobs filtering” scenario if needed for regression.

Notes:
- Keep the information density high, but never sacrifice clarity.
- Make sure `--no-animations` still looks good (no “blank” experience).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:17.395269743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:09:37.537374290Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:09:37.537306804Z","close_reason":"All child tasks complete: table/list, actions, filtering/sorting, progress viz, details pane, timer/stopwatch, paginator. Jobs page is feature-complete.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","page"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dr6","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:17.423951863Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1dsp","title":"Bubbletea: join sync event loop threads on shutdown","description":"# Bubbletea Sync Thread Joining

## Problem
In program.rs, the synchronous event loop (run_with_writer -> event_loop) spawns
three background threads via std::thread::spawn():

1. **External message forwarder** (line ~628-635): Reads from external_rx channel
   and forwards to the main tx channel. Loop breaks when ext_rx disconnects or
   send fails.

2. **Input parser thread** (line ~639-670): Reads raw bytes from stdin, parses
   terminal escape sequences via InputParser, and sends KeyMsg/MouseMsg to tx.
   Loop breaks when stdin read returns 0 or send fails.

3. **Command execution thread** (line ~878-910): Receives Cmd from a channel,
   executes them (which may spawn sub-threads via rayon or std::thread), and
   sends results back to tx. Loop breaks when cmd_rx disconnects.

None of these threads' JoinHandles are stored or joined. When run_with_writer()
returns, the main thread exits and the OS terminates these threads mid-execution.

## Why It Matters
- If a command execution is writing to a file when the process exits, the write
  may be truncated (data loss).
- In SSH mode (wish crate), the process doesn't exit per-connection — leaked
  threads accumulate across sessions.
- Spawned threads that outlive their channels will log "dropped — receiver
  disconnected" warnings, polluting logs.

## Solution
Store JoinHandles from all three thread::spawn() calls. After the event loop
returns, signal threads to stop (drop senders/receivers), then join with a
reasonable timeout (e.g., 2 seconds). If a thread doesn't stop in time, log a
warning and detach (don't block shutdown indefinitely).

## Key Files
- crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs (sync event loop path)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All three spawned threads tracked via JoinHandle
- Threads joined during shutdown with timeout
- Existing tests pass (cargo test -p bubbletea)
- No resource leaks on normal shutdown path","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:37:28.083860624Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T23:48:55.993207792Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dsp","depends_on_id":"bd-1s5p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:37:28.107565580Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":33,"issue_id":"bd-1dsp","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETION CRITERIA: This work stream is complete when BOTH subtasks are done:
1. bd-3dmx: Capture JoinHandles (MUST be done first)
2. bd-3azk: Join threads during shutdown (depends on bd-3dmx)
Plus the testing bead bd-1327 must pass.","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:32:20Z"},{"id":48,"issue_id":"bd-1dsp","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"PARALLELIZATION NOTE:
bd-3dmx (capture handles) and bd-3azk (join threads) are SEQUENTIAL - they modify the same function. bd-3azk depends on bd-3dmx.

After both are complete:
- bd-1327 (tests) can run
- Then bd-1azg (E2E) validates the full flow

CRITICAL PATH: bd-3dmx → bd-3azk → bd-1327 → bd-1azg","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:48:55Z"}]}

## Problem
In program.rs, the synchronous event loop (run_with_writer -> event_loop) spawns
three background threads via std::thread::spawn():

1. **External message forwarder** (line ~628-635): Reads from external_rx channel
   and forwards to the main tx channel. Loop breaks when ext_rx disconnects or
   send fails.

2. **Input parser thread** (line ~639-670): Reads raw bytes from stdin, parses
   terminal escape sequences via InputParser, and sends KeyMsg/MouseMsg to tx.
   Loop breaks when stdin read returns 0 or send fails.

3. **Command execution thread** (line ~878-910): Receives Cmd from a channel,
   executes them (which may spawn sub-threads via rayon or std::thread), and
   sends results back to tx. Loop breaks when cmd_rx disconnects.

None of these threads' JoinHandles are stored or joined. When run_with_writer()
returns, the main thread exits and the OS terminates these threads mid-execution.

## Why It Matters
- If a command execution is writing to a file when the process exits, the write
  may be truncated (data loss).
- In SSH mode (wish crate), the process doesn't exit per-connection — leaked
  threads accumulate across sessions.
- Spawned threads that outlive their channels will log "dropped — receiver
  disconnected" warnings, polluting logs.

## Solution
Store JoinHandles from all three thread::spawn() calls. After the event loop
returns, signal threads to stop (drop senders/receivers), then join with a
reasonable timeout (e.g., 2 seconds). If a thread doesn't stop in time, log a
warning and detach (don't block shutdown indefinitely).

## Key Files
- crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs (sync event loop path)

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-1fxb","title":"Logs: filtering + search (level/service/query)","description":"Add filtering + search controls to the Logs page.

Why:
- Logs are only useful when you can slice them quickly.
- This is also a great way to showcase bubbles textinput + the app’s focus/keybinding model.

Filters to implement:
- Level selector (e.g., TRACE/DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR)
- Service selector (from demo domain model)
- Free-text query (substring or simple matcher)

Performance requirements:
- Filtering should feel instant.
- Avoid re-rendering/parsing the entire log history on every keystroke.
- Prefer an incremental or pre-indexed filter model that can be unit tested.

UX/polish:
- Highlight matches in the viewport (optional).
- Bracketed paste should work in the query input.
- Show an inline summary: "123 lines (12 matches)".

Acceptance:
- Filtering feels instant and scales to a large buffer.
- Covered by unit tests at the filtering-engine layer.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:38.782877999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:55:30.352469043Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:55:30.352332649Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logs","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-2qx1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:38.799160009Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-3jg2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:41.631552397Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:39.044747386Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1fxl","title":"Interactions: focus/blur awareness","description":"Use `FocusMsg`/`BlurMsg` to improve UX and avoid wasting resources when the terminal is unfocused.

Why:
- Focus reporting is an advanced terminal feature that bubbletea supports; the demo should prove it.
- Many TUIs burn CPU animating off-screen. We should behave like a polished app.

Behavior:
- Visual indicator:
  - show a subtle "unfocused" state in the header/status bar (icon + text)
  - reduce contrast of animated/active elements when unfocused
- Optional resource savings:
  - pause or throttle animations
  - pause or slow the background simulation tick

Constraints:
- Must not break determinism in tests:
  - focus/blur should be injectable as messages
  - headless runs should default to focused unless scenario changes it

Acceptance:
- App clearly communicates focus state.
- Unfocused mode avoids needless animation work without breaking the user’s mental model.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:36.412081335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:55:00.660400674Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:55:00.660271694Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","focus","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:36.434141459Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:59.432221685Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:00.706143825Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-wbyt","title":"bubbletea: Track async batch commands with TaskTracker for clean shutdown","description":"# Task: Track Async Batch Commands with TaskTracker

## Problem Statement
In the async event loop, batch commands use bare tokio::spawn() instead of
the TaskTracker:

```rust
// Lines 1293-1303
if let Some(batch) = msg.downcast::<BatchMsg>() {
    for cmd in batch.0 {
        let tx_clone = tx.clone();
        // Note: batch commands don't get tracked individually
        tokio::spawn(async move {  // NOT tracked!
            let cmd_kind: CommandKind = cmd.into();
            if let Some(msg) = cmd_kind.execute().await {
                let _ = tx_clone.send(msg).await;
            }
        });
    }
}
```

The comment acknowledges the limitation: "batch commands don't get tracked individually".

## Impact Analysis
- **Delayed shutdown**: Long-running batch commands continue after quit
- **Resource leaks**: Connections/handles held by batch commands not released
- **Inconsistent behavior**: Regular commands tracked, batch commands not
- **Cancellation ignored**: CancellationToken not passed to batch commands

## Current Shutdown Flow
```
1. User quits -> CancellationToken cancelled
2. TaskTracker waits for tracked tasks
3. 5-second timeout, then force exit
4. Untracked batch commands continue running until process dies
```

## Solution Design

### Track All Batch Commands
```rust
if let Some(batch) = msg.downcast::<BatchMsg>() {
    for cmd in batch.0 {
        let tx_clone = tx.clone();
        let cancel = cancel_token.clone();
        
        tracker.spawn(async move {
            tokio::select! {
                result = async {
                    let cmd_kind: CommandKind = cmd.into();
                    cmd_kind.execute().await
                } => {
                    if let Some(msg) = result {
                        let _ = tx_clone.send(msg).await;
                    }
                }
                _ = cancel.cancelled() => {
                    // Command cancelled during shutdown
                    tracing::debug!("Batch command cancelled during shutdown");
                }
            }
        });
    }
}
```

### Benefits
1. All tasks tracked for clean shutdown
2. Cancellation propagated to batch commands
3. Consistent behavior with regular commands
4. Better resource cleanup

## Implementation Steps
1. Pass TaskTracker to batch command handling
2. Pass CancellationToken to batch commands
3. Wrap command execution in select! for cancellation
4. Update tests to verify shutdown behavior
5. Add integration test for batch command cancellation

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn batch_commands_cancelled_on_shutdown() {
    let completed = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));
    let completed_clone = completed.clone();
    
    // Create batch with slow command
    let slow_cmd = AsyncCmd::new(async move {
        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(10)).await;
        completed_clone.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);
        None
    });
    
    let program = Program::new(TestModel)
        .with_async();
    
    // Start program and immediately quit
    let handle = tokio::spawn(async move {
        program.run_async().await
    });
    
    // Send batch command then quit
    program.send(Command::batch(vec![slow_cmd]));
    program.quit();
    
    // Should complete quickly, not wait 10 seconds
    let result = tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(2), handle).await;
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    
    // Command should NOT have completed
    assert!(!completed.load(Ordering::SeqCst));
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Batch commands tracked via TaskTracker
- [ ] Cancellation signal propagated to batch commands
- [ ] Clean shutdown doesn't wait for batch commands indefinitely
- [ ] No regression in batch command execution
- [ ] Tests verify cancellation behavior","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:21:38.899976369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:30:52.223725910Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:30:52.223656361Z","close_reason":"Replaced bare tokio::spawn with tracker.spawn for async batch commands in handle_command_tracked. Batch sub-commands now receive CancellationToken via tokio::select!, ensuring clean shutdown. TaskTracker and cancel_token are cloned before the outer spawn to be available to batch sub-commands. Sequence commands were already inline (no change needed). All 164 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","bubbletea","shutdown","task-tracking"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wbyt","depends_on_id":"bd-2pex","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:21:38.924547759Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-xnq2","title":"Demo Showcase: unit tests for RwLock poison recovery","description":"# Unit Tests for RwLock Poison Recovery

## Purpose
Verify that the poison-safe RwLock mechanism actually recovers from poisoned
locks and logs appropriate warnings.

## Test Cases

### 1. test_poison_recovery_on_read
Simulate a panic while holding write lock, then verify read still works.

```rust
#[test]
fn test_poison_recovery_on_read() {
    use std::sync::RwLock;
    let lock = RwLock::new(42);
    
    // Poison the lock by panicking while holding write guard
    let _ = std::panic::catch_unwind(std::panic::AssertUnwindSafe(|| {
        let _guard = lock.write().unwrap();
        panic\!("intentional panic to poison lock");
    }));
    
    // Lock should be poisoned
    assert\!(lock.read().is_err());
    
    // With our helper, read should still work
    // (parking_lot: lock.read() works directly)
    // (extension trait: lock.read_or_recover() works)
}
```

### 2. test_poison_recovery_on_write
Same as above but verify write access recovers.

### 3. test_poison_recovery_logs_warning
Verify that recovering from poison logs a warning (for extension trait approach).

```rust
#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn test_poison_recovery_logs_warning() {
    // Poison lock
    // Call read_or_recover()
    // Verify logs contain "RwLock poisoned" or similar
}
```

### 4. test_parking_lot_never_poisons (if using parking_lot)
Demonstrate that parking_lot::RwLock does not poison on panic.

### 5. test_multiple_poison_recoveries
Verify repeated poison-recovery cycles dont corrupt data.

## Integration Test

### 6. test_page_survives_panic_in_update
Simulate a panic in LogsPage::update() while holding lock.
Verify subsequent view() calls still work.

## Key Files
- crates/demo_showcase/src/util.rs (if creating extension trait)
- crates/demo_showcase/src/pages/logs.rs (add integration test)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All test cases implemented and passing
- Tests prove poison recovery works
- Tests use tracing_test to verify warning logs
- No new panics introduced in test suite","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:29:07.076268710Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T23:48:12.054486827Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","reliability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xnq2","depends_on_id":"bd-3v3x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:29:57.756657458Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xnq2","depends_on_id":"bd-ykth","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:29:07.096876523Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":45,"issue_id":"bd-xnq2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CONDITIONAL TEST CASES:
If using parking_lot::RwLock (recommended):
- Skip test cases 1-3 (poison recovery) since parking_lot never poisons
- KEEP test case 4 (test_parking_lot_never_poisons) as the primary verification
- Skip test case 5 (multiple poison recoveries)
- KEEP test case 6 (integration test - verify pages work after simulated panic)

If using extension trait:
- ALL test cases apply
- Test case 3 MUST verify warning log appears

The test file should use cfg attributes to conditionally compile tests based on which approach is used.","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:48:12Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-xnq2","title":"Demo Showcase: unit tests for RwLock poison recovery","description":"# Unit Tests for RwLock Poison Recovery

## Purpose
Verify that the poison-safe RwLock mechanism actually recovers from poisoned
locks and logs appropriate warnings.

## Test Cases

### 1. test_poison_recovery_on_read
Simulate a panic while holding write lock, then verify read still works.

```rust
#[test]
fn test_poison_recovery_on_read() {
    use std::sync::RwLock;
    let lock = RwLock::new(42);
    
    // Poison the lock by panicking while holding write guard
    let _ = std::panic::catch_unwind(std::panic::AssertUnwindSafe(|| {
        let _guard = lock.write().unwrap();
        panic\!("intentional panic to poison lock");
    }));
    
    // Lock should be poisoned
    assert\!(lock.read().is_err());
    
    // With our helper, read should still work
    // (parking_lot: lock.read() works directly)
    // (extension trait: lock.read_or_recover() works)
}
```

### 2. test_poison_recovery_on_write
Same as above but verify write access recovers.

### 3. test_poison_recovery_logs_warning
Verify that recovering from poison logs a warning (for extension trait approach).

```rust
#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn test_poison_recovery_logs_warning() {
    // Poison lock
    // Call read_or_recover()
    // Verify logs contain "RwLock poisoned" or similar
}
```

### 4. test_parking_lot_never_poisons (if using parking_lot)
Demonstrate that parking_lot::RwLock does not poison on panic.

### 5. test_multiple_poison_recoveries
Verify repeated poison-recovery cycles dont corrupt data.

## Integration Test

### 6. test_page_survives_panic_in_update
Simulate a panic in LogsPage::update() while holding lock.
Verify subsequent view() calls still work.

## Key Files
- crates/demo_showcase/src/util.rs (if creating extension trait)
- crates/demo_showcase/src/pages/logs.rs (add integration test)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All test cases implemented and passing
- Tests prove poison recovery works
- Tests use tracing_test to verify warning logs
- No new panics introduced in test suite","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:29:07.076268710Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T01:20:43.384945021Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","reliability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xnq2","depends_on_id":"bd-3v3x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:29:57.756657458Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xnq2","depends_on_id":"bd-ykth","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:29:07.096876523Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":45,"issue_id":"bd-xnq2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CONDITIONAL TEST CASES:
If using parking_lot::RwLock (recommended):
- Skip test cases 1-3 (poison recovery) since parking_lot never poisons
- KEEP test case 4 (test_parking_lot_never_poisons) as the primary verification
- Skip test case 5 (multiple poison recoveries)
- KEEP test case 6 (integration test - verify pages work after simulated panic)

If using extension trait:
- ALL test cases apply
- Test case 3 MUST verify warning log appears

The test file should use cfg attributes to conditionally compile tests based on which approach is used.","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:48:12Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-y3c8","title":"demo_showcase data: background simulation engine (tick/every)","description":"Implement a background simulation that updates data over time.

Use:
- bubbletea tick/every commands (and async equivalents if enabled) to drive state changes.

Testability requirement (critical):
- The simulation must be drivable *without real sleeps*.
  - Prefer a `Tick` message that carries `Instant`/frame count.
  - In production, schedule ticks with `tick/every`.
  - In unit/e2e tests, inject `Tick` messages directly (ProgramSimulator / custom IO) to advance time deterministically.

Behaviors:
- Jobs progress and complete/fail.
- New log lines appear.
- Service health flaps occasionally.
- Deployments start/finish.

Acceptance:
- UI feels alive and realistic without being chaotic; changes drive visible UI updates.
- Tests can advance the simulation 1000 ticks in milliseconds.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:51.780149859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:03:35.524175692Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:03:35.524111703Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-y3c8","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:51.796613145Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-y3c8","depends_on_id":"bd-3bz7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:49.460322408Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-y3c8","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:50.740656518Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-yghg","title":"Animations: loading states + micro-interactions","description":"Add micro-interactions and loading states that make the demo feel alive and premium.

Why:
- Small motion + state feedback is what separates a toy TUI from a "real product".
- This also stress-tests the animation subsystem and tick scheduling.

Examples to implement (pick a few that fit the UI):
- Loading spinners + skeleton/placeholder content for:
  - Docs loading
  - Jobs list refresh
  - File preview loading
- Subtle animations:
  - toast slide-in/out
  - progress easing
  - pulsing warning indicator for degraded service

Constraints:
- Must respect reduce-motion / `--no-animations`:
  - replace motion with instant state changes and clear static indicators
- Must not increase CPU usage unreasonably.

Acceptance:
- Loading states feel intentional and beautiful.
- No-animations mode still communicates state clearly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:59.479257020Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:07:27.954086140Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:07:27.954019365Z","close_reason":"Implemented loading state components module with: LoadingSpinner (themed animated spinner with label, 5 style variants), SkeletonLine/SkeletonBlock (placeholder content with optional pulse animation), LoadingOverlay (centered loading screen), PulsingIndicator (attention-grabbing warning pulse). All components respect --no-animations and REDUCE_MOTION. 20 unit tests passing. Page-level integration is ready for individual page beads.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["anim","demo_showcase","spinner"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yghg","depends_on_id":"bd-2fnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:59.534704063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yghg","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:08.114572604Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yghg","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:09.336208750Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
